title: Nutanix CVM (Controller VM) Resource and Service Health (Per-Service Deep-Dive)
exclusions: This is not the TA-only CVM up-or-down check in UC-19.2.7, not the cluster 0–100 health roll-up in UC-19.2.25, not Prism Central management plane reachability in UC-19.2.12, and not raw physical disk RMAs in UC-19.2.5 (though disk health can show up in Stargate and oplog). It does not cover guest VM guest OS, pure network ToR only failures without CVM nix, Files, Objects, or Era at application scope, or a fictional `nutanix:cvm:metrics` sourcetype. Prism Central CVMs use a different service list; use a separate `pc_service_owner` lookup and feed rather than the PE `expected_services` set.
evidence: A saved search such as `ss_uc_19_2_14_cvm_service_and_pressure_v1` on a two- to five-minute schedule with a PDF or CSV of the final `table` row, `freeze_active`, and `expected_services_missing` archived to a restricted `idx_evidence` on P1, plus a HEC poller or SSH wrapper log, demonstrates four real data families and lookup governance, not a stub `nutanix:cvm:metrics` string.
dataSources: PRIMARY: `sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:genesis_status` from a Splunk_TA_nix (Splunkbase 833) scripted input or equivalent that SSHs as `nutanix@<cvm_ip>` to each Controller VM and runs `genesis status`, wrapping each service line in JSON and POSTing to HEC (one event per CVM per service per sample; fields: `cluster_name` / `clusterName`, `cvm_ip` / `cvmIp`, `service_name` / `serviceName`, `state` or `status` in {`running`, `DOWN`, `kforwarding` for Cassandra}, `restart_count` / `restartCount`). PRIMARY: `sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:status` from a custom Python 3 poller (stdlib `ssl.create_default_context`, `urllib.request`) to GET `https://<cluster_vip>:9440/PrismGateway/services/rest/v1/cluster/status` (basic auth for lab; in production a service user with least privilege) — parse JSON `serviceStatusList` / `service_status_list` per CVM with `Stargate`, `Cassandra`, `Curator`, `Zookeeper`, `Pithos`, `Cerebro`, `Genesis`, `Prism`, `Aplos`, `Mantle`, `Acropolis`, `Uhura` plus `cpuLoad` / `cpu_load`, `memoryUsageBytes` / `memory_usage_bytes`, `memory_total_bytes` / `totalMemoryBytes` when the poller computes ratios. PRIMARY: `sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:stargate_stats` from a poller to GET `https://<pe>:9440/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/hosts/{host_uuid}/stats` and capture `controller_oplog_drain_outstanding` and `oplog_outstanding_drain_count`. CVM host metrics: `index=os` with `sourcetype=cpu` (`pctIdle` for `CPU=all`), `vmstat` (`pctIowait`, `memUsedPct` or `mem_used_pct`), `iostat` (`await`, `tps`), and `df` (used percent) from Splunk_TA_nix on each CVM. OPTIONAL: `sourcetype=nutanix:cluster:alerts` (Splunk_TA_nutanix, Splunkbase 5076) with fields such as `title` / `alertTitle` for `StargateDown`, `CassandraDown`, `CuratorDown`, `ServiceRestartedFrequently` — coalesce field names. Governance: `lookups/cvm_service_owner.csv` with `cluster_name`, `cvm_ip`, `expected_services` (comma-separated), `business_service`, `owner_email`, `change_freeze_until` loaded as `cvm_service_owner` (two key columns) in `transforms.conf`.
app: Splunk_TA_nix ([Splunkbase 833](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)) for scripted `ssh` and `cpu` / `vmstat` / `iostat` / `df` on CVMs. Splunk_TA_nutanix ([Splunkbase 5076](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)) for `nutanix:cluster:alerts` when the modular input is enabled. A custom app contains HEC pollers, `cvm_service_owner.csv`, and `default/props.conf` for the HEC JSON sourcetypes. Optional: `premiumApps`.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.14 — CVM per-service: genesis_status, nutanix:cvm:status, stargate_stats, nix, nutanix:cluster:alerts, cvm_service_owner, freeze. earliest=-30m@m; coalesce; AND OR; OPLOG_WARN; replace index= names.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:genesis_status earliest=-30m@m
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, ""))
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, cvmIp, service_vmaddress, host_ip, hostIp, ""))
  | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name, serviceName, skey, ""))))
  | eval st=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(state, service_state, status, serviceStatus, ""))))
  | eval rcount=tonumber(toString(coalesce(restart_count, restarts, restartCount, "0")), 10)
  | eval is_run=if(((st IN ("running", "up", "kstarting")) OR ((service_name=="cassandra") AND (match(st, "(kforwarding|forwarding)")))), 1, 0)
  | eval is_down=if(((len(cvm_ip)>0) AND (len(service_name)>0) AND (is_run=0)), 1, 0)
  | stats
      count(eval(is_down=1)) AS services_down_count
      values(eval(if(is_down=1, service_name, null()))) AS services_down_list
      sum(rcount) AS restart_count_30m
      values(eval(if((rcount>3), service_name, null()))) AS crash_looping_services
      values(eval(if((is_run=1), service_name, null()))) AS all_up
      max(eval(if((service_name=="stargate") AND (is_down=1),1,0))) AS _stg
      max(eval(if((service_name=="cassandra") AND (is_down=1),1,0))) AS _cas
      max(eval(if((match(service_name, "(zookeeper|zoo_keeper)")) AND (is_down=1), 1, 0))) AS _zk
    BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:status earliest=-30m@m
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, ""))
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, cvmIp, service_vmaddress, backplane_ip, backplaneIp, ""))
  | eval mem_u=tonumber(toString(coalesce(memory_usage_bytes, memoryUsageBytes, "0")), 10)
  | eval mem_t=tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_memory_bytes, mem_total_bytes, memTotalBytes, memory_total_bytes, memoryTotalBytes, "0")), 10)
  | eval mem_used_max_pct=if((mem_t>0), round((mem_u*100.0)/mem_t,1), null())
  | where (isnotnull(cvm_ip)) AND (len(trim(tostring(cvm_ip)))>0)
  | stats max(mem_used_max_pct) AS mem_used_max_pct BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:stargate_stats earliest=-30m@m
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, ""))
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, cvmIp, service_vmaddress, host_ip, hostIp, ""))
  | eval oplog_drain_outstanding_max=tonumber(toString(coalesce(
    controller_oplog_drain_outstanding, controllerOplogDrainOutstanding,
    oplog_outstanding_drain_count, oplogOutstandingDrainCount, "0")), 10)
  | where (isnotnull(cvm_ip)) AND (len(trim(tostring(cvm_ip)))>0)
  | stats max(oplog_drain_outstanding_max) AS oplog_drain_outstanding_max BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:cluster:alerts earliest=-30m@m
  | eval ttitle=toString(coalesce(title, alertTitle, alert_name, name, ""))
  | where ttitle=="StargateDown" OR ttitle=="CassandraDown" OR ttitle=="CuratorDown" OR ttitle=="ServiceRestartedFrequently"
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, cvmIp, service_vmaddress, param_cvm, affected_cvm, ""))
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, cluster, ""))
  | where (isnotnull(cvm_ip)) AND (len(trim(tostring(cvm_ip)))>0)
  | stats count AS alert_hint BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
    [ search index=os (sourcetype=cpu OR sourcetype=vmstat OR sourcetype=iostat OR sourcetype=df) earliest=-30m@m
  | eval host_key=toString(coalesce(host, hostName, dvc, ""))
  | lookup local=true cvm_service_owner cvm_ip AS host_key OUTPUT cvm_ip AS in_scope, cluster_name AS cluster_from_lu
  | where isnotnull(in_scope)
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(in_scope)
  | eval cluster_name=if(isnotnull(cluster_from_lu), toString(cluster_from_lu), toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, _cluster, "")))
  | eval _cpu=if(sourcetype=="cpu" AND (CPU=="all"), tonumber(toString(pctIdle), 10), null())
  | eval _iow=if(sourcetype=="vmstat", tonumber(toString(pctIowait), 10), null())
  | eval _memp=if(sourcetype=="vmstat", tonumber(toString(coalesce(memUsedPct, mem_used_pct, "")), 10), null())
  | eval _aw=if(sourcetype=="iostat", tonumber(toString(coalesce(await, await_ms, "")), 10), null())
  | eval _df=if(sourcetype=="df", tonumber(toString(coalesce(UsePct, use_pct, percent_used, "")), 10), null())
  | eval _memp2=if(isnotnull(_df), 100.0 - _df, _memp)
  | stats min(_cpu) AS _cpu_min max(_iow) AS iowait_max_pct max(_memp2) AS mem_used_max_pct max(_aw) AS iostat_await_max BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval cpu_idle_min=if((isnotnull(_cpu_min) AND (tonumber(_cpu_min)>=0) AND (tonumber(_cpu_min)<=100)), 100.0 - _cpu_min, null())
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
| eval k=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, ""))."#".toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, ""))
| stats
    first(services_down_list) AS services_down_list
    first(all_up) AS all_up
    max(services_down_count) AS services_down_count
    max(_stg) AS _stg
    max(_cas) AS _cas
    max(_zk) AS _zk
    max(restart_count_30m) AS restart_count_30m
    first(crash_looping_services) AS crash_looping_services
    max(mem_used_max_pct) AS mem_used_max_pct
    max(iowait_max_pct) AS iowait_max_pct
    min(cpu_idle_min) AS cpu_idle_min
    max(iostat_await_max) AS iostat_await_max
    max(oplog_drain_outstanding_max) AS oplog_drain_outstanding_max
    max(alert_hint) AS alert_hint
  BY k, cluster_name, cvm_ip
| fillnull value=0 services_down_count restart_count_30m
| fillnull value=0 _stg _cas _zk
| lookup local=true cvm_service_owner cluster_name, cvm_ip OUTPUT expected_services, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
| eval sdl=if((isnotnull(services_down_list)) AND (mvcount(services_down_list)>0), lower(mvjoin(services_down_list, "|")), "")
| eval has_crash=if((isnotnull(crash_looping_services)) AND (mvcount(crash_looping_services)>0), 1, 0)
| eval has_stg=if((match(sdl, "(stargate)") OR (_stg=1)), 1, 0)
| eval has_cas=if((match(sdl, "(cassandra)") OR (_cas=1)), 1, 0)
| eval has_zk=if((match(sdl, "(zookeeper|zoo_keeper)")) OR (_zk=1), 1, 0)
| eval multi_down=if((tonumber(services_down_count)>=2),1,0)
| eval tdn=tonumber(services_down_count)
| eval sev0=if(((has_stg=1) OR (has_cas=1) OR (has_zk=1) OR (multi_down=1) OR (has_crash=1)), "critical", null())
| eval sev0=if(isnull(sev0), if((((tdn=1) AND (has_stg=0) AND (has_cas=0) AND (has_zk=0) AND (len(sdl)>0)) OR (tonumber(iowait_max_pct)>50) OR (tonumber(mem_used_max_pct)>95)), "high", null()), sev0)
| eval OPLOG_WARN=5000000.0
| eval sev0=if(isnull(sev0), if(((tonumber(restart_count_30m)>1) OR (tonumber(iowait_max_pct)>30) OR ((isnotnull(oplog_drain_outstanding_max)) AND (oplog_drain_outstanding_max>OPLOG_WARN))), "medium", null()), sev0)
| eval sev0=if(isnull(sev0), if((tonumber(restart_count_30m)=1), "low", null()), sev0)
| eval sev0=if(isnull(sev0), "ok", sev0)
| eval severity_bucket=sev0
| eval cft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cft) AND (now() < cft) AND (len(trim(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0)), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=if(((freeze_active=1) AND (severity_bucket!="critical")), case(severity_bucket=="high", "medium", severity_bucket=="medium", "low", severity_bucket=="low", "ok", 1=1, severity_bucket), severity_bucket)
| eval severity_bucket=sev0
| eval toks=if((isnotnull(expected_services)) AND (len(trim(tostring(expected_services)))>0), split(replace(replace(tostring(expected_services), " ", ""), ",,", ","), ","), null())
| mvexpand toks
| eval exp_t=lower(trim(tostring(toks)))
| where (len(exp_t)>0)
| eval miss=if((isnull(mvcount(all_up))) OR (mvcount(all_up)=0), exp_t, if((isnotnull(mvfind(all_up, exp_t))), null(), exp_t))
| stats first(cluster_name) AS cluster_name, first(cvm_ip) AS cvm_ip, first(services_down_list) AS services_down_list, first(services_down_count) AS services_down_count, first(crash_looping_services) AS crash_looping_services, first(restart_count_30m) AS restart_count_30m, first(cpu_idle_min) AS cpu_idle_min, first(mem_used_max_pct) AS mem_used_max_pct, first(iowait_max_pct) AS iowait_max_pct, first(iostat_await_max) AS iostat_await_max, first(oplog_drain_outstanding_max) AS oplog_drain_outstanding_max, first(severity_bucket) AS severity_bucket, first(freeze_active) AS freeze_active, first(business_service) AS business_service, first(owner_email) AS owner_email, first(alert_hint) AS alert_hint, values(miss) AS expected_services_missing BY k
| sort 0 - severity_bucket, services_down_count, cvm_ip
| table cluster_name, cvm_ip, services_down_count, services_down_list, crash_looping_services, restart_count_30m, cpu_idle_min, mem_used_max_pct, iowait_max_pct, oplog_drain_outstanding_max, expected_services_missing, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
description: Correlates per-Controller-VM internal Nutanix service state (Genesis-style lines and optional cluster status HEC), restart dynamics, oplog back-pressure from Stargate host stats, and CVM `cpu` / `vmstat` / `iostat` / `df` from Splunk_TA_nix, enriches with `cvm_service_owner` for `expected_services` and `change_freeze_until`, and classifies a five-bucket `severity_bucket` with freeze demotion that never silences a true critical. This is the per-service and per-CVM resource deep-dive that complements UC-19.2.7 (CVM availability and TA `nutanix:cluster:services_status`) and UC-19.2.25 (cluster health score) by exposing restart loops, oplog drain risk, and Linux guest iowait or memory stress that a cluster-level score can miss.
value: A Controller VM co-processes storage and metadata for its hypervisor. When internal services wobble, guest I/O, metadata safety, and rebuild behavior shift across the cluster in ways a single headline number can mask. This use case makes `services_down_list`, `crash_looping_services` from per-service `restart_count`, Stargate oplog backlog, and CVM iowait and memory visible on one `cluster_name, cvm_ip` row, with `expected_services_missing` when your governance CSV says a daemon should be present. Owner and change-freeze columns route incidents without down-classifying Stargate, Cassandra, or Zookeeper `DOWN` or crash-loop results during a change freeze for lower tiers only.
implementation: Generate SSH keys and deploy to each CVM; run Splunk_TA_nix scripted `genesis status` to HEC; deploy Python 3 (stdlib) pollers for `/v1/cluster/status` and per-host v2.0 `hosts/{uuid}/stats`; install nix `cpu` / `vmstat` / `iostat` on each CVM; enable 5076 alerts if desired; create `cvm_service_owner` with a two-key `transforms.conf` stanza. Schedule the saved search every two to five minutes with `earliest=-30m@m`, throttle on `k`, and PagerDuty or ServiceNow routing per Step 4. The `mvexpand` / `values(miss)` block implements `expected_services` minus the running set from `all_up` — simplify `miss` in dev if you see redundant `null()` (some builds only need `mvfind(all_up, exp_t)`).
visualization: (1) Single value: CVMs with Stargate, Cassandra, or Zookeeper in `services_down_list`. (2) Table: `cluster_name`, `cvm_ip`, `services_down_count`, `services_down_list`, `crash_looping_services`, `restart_count_30m`, `cpu_idle_min`, `mem_used_max_pct`, `iowait_max_pct`, `oplog_drain_outstanding_max`, `expected_services_missing`, `severity_bucket`, `freeze_active`, `business_service`, `owner_email`. (3) Timechart: `restart_count_30m` and `services_down_count` by `cvm_ip`. (4) Scatter: `iowait_max_pct` versus `mem_used_max_pct` by CVM. (5) Panel: `nutanix:cluster:alerts` for service-down titles, keyed by `cvm_ip` when present.

references.title: Nutanix Developer — Prism Gateway RAPIs reference
Nutanix — Technical documentation and support (portal home)
Nutanix Developer — product and API home

knownFalsePositives: A rolling AOS upgrade intentionally cycles Stargate and other Genesis-supervised services; pair spikes in `restart_count_30m` to `cluster.upgrade_in_progress` in your `nutanix:cvm:status` HEC and the change record before auto-paging. `ergon` and `minerva_cvm` in `services_down_list` on some AOS lines are often transient worker processes; document them before they drive tickets. A single poll where Prism still shows `UP` but Genesis shows `DOWN` is usually a thirty-second UI cache; compare a second poll. `nutanix:cluster:alerts` can lag a healthy `genesis status` on the wire by one modular-input interval. Labs that use FQDN in `host` for nix but only IP in the lookup return empty nix subsearch rows until the lookup or `host` is normalized. Stretched or witness designs can make one CVM’s `iowait` look bad during rebuilds while service rows are still `running`; treat as capacity context when `services_down_list` is empty. Spurious oplog `max` on a new poller is often a field rename—re-run `| fieldsummary` after each AOS minor.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

The Controller VM (CVM) is a virtual machine on each hypervisor node that runs the distributed storage, metadata, and control daemons. Losing a CVM means that node’s local I/O and metadata path is impaired until the cluster rebalances. A cluster-aggregate health score (UC-19.2.25) can still look healthy while a single CVM is in a Stargate restart loop; UC-19.2.7 answers availability from the vendor TA services stream, while this use case is the per-service, per-process deep dive with `restart_count`, Linux `pctIowait`, and oplog back-pressure. You need four data paths: (1) SSH or scripted `genesis status` to `nutanix:cvm:genesis_status` with PIDs, state, and restarts; (2) GET `/PrismGateway/services/rest/v1/cluster/status` to `nutanix:cvm:status` for `serviceStatusList` and memory or CPU; (3) GET `/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/hosts/{host_uuid}/stats` to `nutanix:cvm:stargate_stats` for oplog; (4) `Splunk_TA_nix` on each CVM for `cpu`, `vmstat`, `iostat`, and `df` in `index=os`, scoped by `cvm_service_owner` so only CVMs are in scope. Optional `nutanix:cluster:alerts` cross-checks StargateDown-style titles. Governance CSV `cvm_service_owner.csv` keys `cluster_name` and `cvm_ip` to `expected_services` (comma-separated, tuned per AOS), `business_service`, `owner_email`, and `change_freeze_until`.

CVM service primer: Stargate serves guest I/O; if Stargate is DOWN on a CVM, that CVM cannot serve guest I/O. Cassandra stores vDisk and oplog metadata; losing more than half of the replication factor of Cassandra nodes blocks metadata paths. Curator does background repair and scan work; if Curator is down, repair queues stall. Zookeeper holds leadership and config; loss of quorum can freeze the cluster. Genesis starts and supervises the stack; a Genesis crash is severe. Prism is management API; Prism down does not always stop the data path. Cerebro is async replication. Pithos is vDisk identity. Aplos, Mantle, Acropolis, and Uhura cover API, identity, AHV, and VM RPC—expect name differences by AOS.

Signatures you care about: crash-loop = `restart_count` rising and `state` flickering in thirty minutes. Pressure = `pctIowait` sustained above roughly thirty, memory used high in the CVM, or oplog `controller_oplog_drain_outstanding` (or `oplog_outstanding_drain_count` per AOS) high versus your baseline, meaning the SSD oplog is not draining to capacity in time. `cvm_service_owner` gives `expected_services` so the SPL can list `expected_services_missing` with `mvexpand` and `mvfind` against the running set `all_up` from `genesis_status`.

`default/transforms.conf`:

```ini
[cvm_service_owner]
filename = cvm_service_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = true
min_matches = 0
```

Volume: one four-node cluster, twelve services, sixty-second genesis sampling: plan roughly four CVMs times twelve events per minute for genesis alone, plus pollers and nix, before index replication. Budget index size accordingly.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

Generate an SSH key pair; store the private key in a vault, never in `inputs.conf`. Push the public key to `~nutanix/.ssh/authorized_keys` on each CVM. If cluster lockdown or external lockdown blocks automation SSH, add keys through Prism or an approved runbook, or use API-only until keys work.

`Splunk_TA_nix` (Splunkbase 833) `script://` to run:
`ssh -i /opt/splunk/etc/auth/splunk_cvm_id_rsa -o BatchMode=yes -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new nutanix@<cvm_ip> 'export PATH="/usr/local/nutanix/bin:$PATH" ; genesis status'`
Parse into JSON and POST to HEC as `sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:genesis_status` every sixty seconds, one line per CVM per service. Chunk posts per CVM to stay under the one-megabyte HEC line limit on large `genesis status` output.

Cluster status: Python 3 with `ssl.create_default_context` and `urllib.request` to GET `https://<cluster_vip>:9440/PrismGateway/services/rest/v1/cluster/status` with `Accept: application/json`. Handle 401 and 503 with back-off. Emit HEC to `nutanix:cvm:status` with `cvm_ip`, `cpu_load`, `memoryUsageBytes` or `totalMemoryBytes` as your poller maps them, every thirty seconds.

Stargate stats: from `nutanix:host` take `host_uuid` for each CVM, poll `GET /PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/hosts/{host_uuid}/stats`, extract oplog and IOPS; emit `nutanix:cvm:stargate_stats` with `cvm_ip` and `cluster_name` coalesced from host inventory, every thirty to sixty seconds.

CVM nix: Universal Forwarder with `cpu`, `vmstat`, `iostat`, `df` to `index=os` at sixty seconds. Ensure the forwarder’s `host` field matches the `cvm_ip` column in the lookup, or add a `props` alias.

`Splunk_TA_nutanix` 5076: enable the input that populates `nutanix:cluster:alerts` and align `index=` with the multisearch. Example HEC for genesis only:

```ini
[http://nutanix_cvm_genesis]
disabled = 0
indexes = hci
sourcetype = nutanix:cvm:genesis_status
token = <REDACTED>
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The saved search should keep the first-line `comment` for audit, use `earliest=-30m@m` on the search or in the schedule time window, and use uppercase AND OR in the rare inline `where` you add. The `multisearch` pulls genesis, status memory percent, oplog, optional TA alerts, and nix, then `stats` merges on `k=cluster_name#cvm_ip`, applies five-tier `severity_bucket`, freeze demotion (never for true `critical` such as Stargate, Cassandra, or Zookeeper down, multi-down, or crash loops), and finishes with `mvexpand` to compute `expected_services_missing` from the lookup’s comma list versus `all_up` from Genesis.

Search walkthrough: each arm coalesces camel and snake; non-genesis arms have null `all_up` until the merge, where `first(all_up)` carries the CVM’s running service multivalue. High is reserved for a single down non-core service, or I/O or memory stress thresholds; critical wins on the three core services or two-or-more down or crash list. The freeze `case` only demotes `high` through `ok`, never a row that is already `critical`.

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.14 — CVM per-service: genesis_status, nutanix:cvm:status, stargate_stats, nix, nutanix:cluster:alerts, cvm_service_owner, freeze. earliest=-30m@m; coalesce; AND OR; OPLOG_WARN; replace index= names.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:genesis_status earliest=-30m@m
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, ""))
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, cvmIp, service_vmaddress, host_ip, hostIp, ""))
  | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name, serviceName, skey, ""))))
  | eval st=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(state, service_state, status, serviceStatus, ""))))
  | eval rcount=tonumber(toString(coalesce(restart_count, restarts, restartCount, "0")), 10)
  | eval is_run=if(((st IN ("running", "up", "kstarting")) OR ((service_name=="cassandra") AND (match(st, "(kforwarding|forwarding)")))), 1, 0)
  | eval is_down=if(((len(cvm_ip)>0) AND (len(service_name)>0) AND (is_run=0)), 1, 0)
  | stats
      count(eval(is_down=1)) AS services_down_count
      values(eval(if(is_down=1, service_name, null()))) AS services_down_list
      sum(rcount) AS restart_count_30m
      values(eval(if((rcount>3), service_name, null()))) AS crash_looping_services
      values(eval(if((is_run=1), service_name, null()))) AS all_up
      max(eval(if((service_name=="stargate") AND (is_down=1),1,0))) AS _stg
      max(eval(if((service_name=="cassandra") AND (is_down=1),1,0))) AS _cas
      max(eval(if((match(service_name, "(zookeeper|zoo_keeper)")) AND (is_down=1), 1, 0))) AS _zk
    BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:status earliest=-30m@m
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, ""))
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, cvmIp, service_vmaddress, backplane_ip, backplaneIp, ""))
  | eval mem_u=tonumber(toString(coalesce(memory_usage_bytes, memoryUsageBytes, "0")), 10)
  | eval mem_t=tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_memory_bytes, mem_total_bytes, memTotalBytes, memory_total_bytes, memoryTotalBytes, "0")), 10)
  | eval mem_used_max_pct=if((mem_t>0), round((mem_u*100.0)/mem_t,1), null())
  | where (isnotnull(cvm_ip)) AND (len(trim(tostring(cvm_ip)))>0)
  | stats max(mem_used_max_pct) AS mem_used_max_pct BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:cvm:stargate_stats earliest=-30m@m
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, ""))
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, cvmIp, service_vmaddress, host_ip, hostIp, ""))
  | eval oplog_drain_outstanding_max=tonumber(toString(coalesce(
    controller_oplog_drain_outstanding, controllerOplogDrainOutstanding,
    oplog_outstanding_drain_count, oplogOutstandingDrainCount, "0")), 10)
  | where (isnotnull(cvm_ip)) AND (len(trim(tostring(cvm_ip)))>0)
  | stats max(oplog_drain_outstanding_max) AS oplog_drain_outstanding_max BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:cluster:alerts earliest=-30m@m
  | eval ttitle=toString(coalesce(title, alertTitle, alert_name, name, ""))
  | where ttitle=="StargateDown" OR ttitle=="CassandraDown" OR ttitle=="CuratorDown" OR ttitle=="ServiceRestartedFrequently"
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, cvmIp, service_vmaddress, param_cvm, affected_cvm, ""))
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, cluster, ""))
  | where (isnotnull(cvm_ip)) AND (len(trim(tostring(cvm_ip)))>0)
  | stats count AS alert_hint BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
    [ search index=os (sourcetype=cpu OR sourcetype=vmstat OR sourcetype=iostat OR sourcetype=df) earliest=-30m@m
  | eval host_key=toString(coalesce(host, hostName, dvc, ""))
  | lookup local=true cvm_service_owner cvm_ip AS host_key OUTPUT cvm_ip AS in_scope, cluster_name AS cluster_from_lu
  | where isnotnull(in_scope)
  | eval cvm_ip=toString(in_scope)
  | eval cluster_name=if(isnotnull(cluster_from_lu), toString(cluster_from_lu), toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, _cluster, "")))
  | eval _cpu=if(sourcetype=="cpu" AND (CPU=="all"), tonumber(toString(pctIdle), 10), null())
  | eval _iow=if(sourcetype=="vmstat", tonumber(toString(pctIowait), 10), null())
  | eval _memp=if(sourcetype=="vmstat", tonumber(toString(coalesce(memUsedPct, mem_used_pct, "")), 10), null())
  | eval _aw=if(sourcetype=="iostat", tonumber(toString(coalesce(await, await_ms, "")), 10), null())
  | eval _df=if(sourcetype=="df", tonumber(toString(coalesce(UsePct, use_pct, percent_used, "")), 10), null())
  | eval _memp2=if(isnotnull(_df), 100.0 - _df, _memp)
  | stats min(_cpu) AS _cpu_min max(_iow) AS iowait_max_pct max(_memp2) AS mem_used_max_pct max(_aw) AS iostat_await_max BY cluster_name, cvm_ip
  | eval cpu_idle_min=if((isnotnull(_cpu_min) AND (tonumber(_cpu_min)>=0) AND (tonumber(_cpu_min)<=100)), 100.0 - _cpu_min, null())
  | eval k=toString(cluster_name)."#".toString(cvm_ip)
]
| eval k=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, ""))."#".toString(coalesce(cvm_ip, ""))
| stats
    first(services_down_list) AS services_down_list
    first(all_up) AS all_up
    max(services_down_count) AS services_down_count
    max(_stg) AS _stg
    max(_cas) AS _cas
    max(_zk) AS _zk
    max(restart_count_30m) AS restart_count_30m
    first(crash_looping_services) AS crash_looping_services
    max(mem_used_max_pct) AS mem_used_max_pct
    max(iowait_max_pct) AS iowait_max_pct
    min(cpu_idle_min) AS cpu_idle_min
    max(iostat_await_max) AS iostat_await_max
    max(oplog_drain_outstanding_max) AS oplog_drain_outstanding_max
    max(alert_hint) AS alert_hint
  BY k, cluster_name, cvm_ip
| fillnull value=0 services_down_count restart_count_30m
| fillnull value=0 _stg _cas _zk
| lookup local=true cvm_service_owner cluster_name, cvm_ip OUTPUT expected_services, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
| eval sdl=if((isnotnull(services_down_list)) AND (mvcount(services_down_list)>0), lower(mvjoin(services_down_list, "|")), "")
| eval has_crash=if((isnotnull(crash_looping_services)) AND (mvcount(crash_looping_services)>0), 1, 0)
| eval has_stg=if((match(sdl, "(stargate)") OR (_stg=1)), 1, 0)
| eval has_cas=if((match(sdl, "(cassandra)") OR (_cas=1)), 1, 0)
| eval has_zk=if((match(sdl, "(zookeeper|zoo_keeper)")) OR (_zk=1), 1, 0)
| eval multi_down=if((tonumber(services_down_count)>=2),1,0)
| eval tdn=tonumber(services_down_count)
| eval sev0=if(((has_stg=1) OR (has_cas=1) OR (has_zk=1) OR (multi_down=1) OR (has_crash=1)), "critical", null())
| eval sev0=if(isnull(sev0), if((((tdn=1) AND (has_stg=0) AND (has_cas=0) AND (has_zk=0) AND (len(sdl)>0)) OR (tonumber(iowait_max_pct)>50) OR (tonumber(mem_used_max_pct)>95)), "high", null()), sev0)
| eval OPLOG_WARN=5000000.0
| eval sev0=if(isnull(sev0), if(((tonumber(restart_count_30m)>1) OR (tonumber(iowait_max_pct)>30) OR ((isnotnull(oplog_drain_outstanding_max)) AND (oplog_drain_outstanding_max>OPLOG_WARN))), "medium", null()), sev0)
| eval sev0=if(isnull(sev0), if((tonumber(restart_count_30m)=1), "low", null()), sev0)
| eval sev0=if(isnull(sev0), "ok", sev0)
| eval severity_bucket=sev0
| eval cft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cft) AND (now() < cft) AND (len(trim(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0)), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=if(((freeze_active=1) AND (severity_bucket!="critical")), case(severity_bucket=="high", "medium", severity_bucket=="medium", "low", severity_bucket=="low", "ok", 1=1, severity_bucket), severity_bucket)
| eval severity_bucket=sev0
| eval toks=if((isnotnull(expected_services)) AND (len(trim(tostring(expected_services)))>0), split(replace(replace(tostring(expected_services), " ", ""), ",,", ","), ","), null())
| mvexpand toks
| eval exp_t=lower(trim(tostring(toks)))
| where (len(exp_t)>0)
| eval miss=if((isnull(mvcount(all_up))) OR (mvcount(all_up)=0), exp_t, if((isnotnull(mvfind(all_up, exp_t))), null(), exp_t))
| stats first(cluster_name) AS cluster_name, first(cvm_ip) AS cvm_ip, first(services_down_list) AS services_down_list, first(services_down_count) AS services_down_count, first(crash_looping_services) AS crash_looping_services, first(restart_count_30m) AS restart_count_30m, first(cpu_idle_min) AS cpu_idle_min, first(mem_used_max_pct) AS mem_used_max_pct, first(iowait_max_pct) AS iowait_max_pct, first(iostat_await_max) AS iostat_await_max, first(oplog_drain_outstanding_max) AS oplog_drain_outstanding_max, first(severity_bucket) AS severity_bucket, first(freeze_active) AS freeze_active, first(business_service) AS business_service, first(owner_email) AS owner_email, first(alert_hint) AS alert_hint, values(miss) AS expected_services_missing BY k
| sort 0 - severity_bucket, services_down_count, cvm_ip
| table cluster_name, cvm_ip, services_down_count, services_down_list, crash_looping_services, restart_count_30m, cpu_idle_min, mem_used_max_pct, iowait_max_pct, oplog_drain_outstanding_max, expected_services_missing, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
```

Schedule every two to five minutes; alert when `severity_bucket` is `critical` or `high` as policy dictates; throttle on `k`. PagerDuty for Stargate, Cassandra, or Zookeeper class rows; ServiceNow P1 for crash loops; Slack for `medium`, email for `low` by default.

Step 3 — Validate

(1) SSH to a CVM, run `genesis status`, and compare to the newest `nutanix:cvm:genesis_status` for that `cvm_ip`. (2) From a CVM, `allssh genesis status` for cross-node. (3) Prism: Home, Hardware, Diagram, CVM, Service Status—allow roughly thirty seconds of cache before disagreeing. (4) `ncli host list` (or current CLI) for CVM inventory versus lookup. (5) `curl -k -u user:pass -H "Accept: application/json" 'https://<cluster_vip>:9440/PrismGateway/services/rest/v1/cluster/status'`. (6) If UI and Splunk disagree, check HEC or TA lag, SSH reachability, or a transient restart. (7) Compare oplog fields from the raw stats API to `nutanix:cvm:stargate_stats`.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard: single value for CVMs with Stargate, Cassandra, or Zookeeper in `services_down_list`; table by `services_down_count` descending; timechart of `restart_count_30m`; scatter `iowait_max_pct` vs `mem_used_max_pct`; list of `nutanix:cluster:alerts` for service-down titles. Route critical Stargate, Cassandra, or Zookeeper to PagerDuty; crash loops to ServiceNow P1; medium to Slack; low to email. ITSI: KPI for `services_down_count` per cluster and CVM resource pressure. Runbooks: Stargate loop—`logs` under `/home/nutanix/data/logs` and disk health (UC-19.2.5); Cassandra—Paxos and token; Zookeeper—quorum and vendor support; memory pressure—balloon, upgrade staging, CVM vRAM.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(a) Empty results: test SSH, lockdown, and key placement. (b) Short service list: AOS version; update `expected_services`. (c) `pctIowait` zero: vmstat not enabled on CVM or host mismatch in lookup. (d) oplog null: add coalesce keys for your AOS. (e) DOWN in Genesis, UP in Prism: short UI cache. (f) AOS upgrade: suppress when `cluster.upgrade_in_progress` is true. (g) Freeze and Stargate: keep demotion from touching `critical` rows. (h) `ergon` and `minerva_cvm` transient downs: document. (i) HEC payload: chunk per CVM. (j) host vs IP: add `EVAL` in `props` to normalize. (k) Prism Central CVMs: separate lookup and do not use PE Stargate logic on a PC CVM.
grandmaExplanation: We look inside the small control computers on each box that run the shared storage and catalog for your virtual machines, not just a single health number for the whole group. We watch for programs that keep restarting, run out of memory, or drown in disk wait, and we tell the right people in time, before the whole group feels slow because one box is struggling.
hardware: Nutanix NX lines including NX-8000, NX-9000, and NX-3000-class nodes; Dell EMC XC and Dell Integrated System; Lenovo ThinkAgile HX; HPE ProLiant, DX, and Apollo; Cisco UCS for Nutanix; Inspur InMerge; Fujitsu PRIMEFLEX; Crystal Group rugged images; any qualified block running supported AOS with a single CVM per node on AHV, ESXi, or Hyper-V. The CVM is a VM on the local hypervisor; losing it blocks the local data path for that node until the cluster reassigns. Document all-flash versus hybrid, stretched versus local, and whether Prism Element is reached directly or via a Prism Central hop for poller design.
